recipe.title: Chocolate orange pots
recipe.description: When only chocolate will do, these pots are sweet, tangy, comforting, silky and utterly delicious. Super quick and easy to make as a weeknight dessert!

ingredients.name: heaped tablespoons Greek or plant-based yoghurt
seasonal fruit
ingredients.note: such as oranges, cherries, strawberries, raspberries, to serve

steps.descr: In a bowl, just cover the dates with boiling kettle...
steps.instructions: In a bowl, just cover the dates with boiling kettle water and leave to soak for 5 minutes, then drain. Toast the hazelnuts in a small frying pan until lightly golden, shaking regularly, then crush in a pestle and mortar until fine.

on_enter.say: In a bowl, just cover the dates with boiling kettle water and leave to soak for 5 minutes, then drain. Toast the hazelnuts in a small frying pan until lightly golden, shaking regularly, then crush in a pestle and mortar until fine.

steps.descr: Put the dates in a blender with the tofu and cocoa
steps.instructions: Put the dates in a blender with the tofu and cocoa. Finely grate in the orange zest, squeeze in the juice, add 1 ice cube, if you have it, and blitz until super-smooth, stopping to scrape down the sides with a spatula a few times.

on_enter.say: Put the dates in a blender with the tofu and cocoa. Finely grate in the orange zest, squeeze in the juice, add 1 ice cube, if you have it, and blitz until super-smooth, stopping to scrape down the sides with a spatula a few times.

steps.descr: Decant the mixture between little pots, glasses or cups,...
steps.instructions: Decant the mixture between little pots, glasses or cups, and either eat right away, or cover and stash in the fridge until you want them (up to 3 days). Serve each pot with a spoonful of yoghurt, a sprinkling of crushed hazelnuts and some extra fruit, of your choice.

on_enter.say: Decant the mixture between little pots, glasses or cups, and either eat right away, or cover and stash in the fridge until you want them (up to 3 days). Serve each pot with a spoonful of yoghurt, a sprinkling of crushed hazelnuts and some extra fruit, of your choice.
